ocr.text: The advancements in medical technology continue to revolutionize patient care, particularly through the development of
innovative devices designed to enhance physiological functions. The data presented in the accompanying table summarizes key
performance metrics of various medical devices, including Pace-Makers and Neural Stimulators, which are critical in managing
chronic conditions. Notably, the throughput capabilities of these devices, measured in kilobits per second (KBPS), indicate their
efficiency in data transmission, with several systems achieving speeds of up to 100 KBPS. This is particularly significant in the
context of real-time monitoring and feedback mechanisms essential for patient safety and treatment efficacy. Additionally, the
table highlights the Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic curve (AUROC) along with its 95% Confidence Interval
(CI) for each device, providing a quantitative measure of diagnostic accuracy. These metrics serve as vital benchmarks for
evaluating the performance of technologies such as Drug-Delivery Systems and Retinal Implants, ultimately guiding clinical
decisions and enhancing therapeutic outcomes in diverse patient populations.
